
### double-free exercise  {.smaller}


:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

```
free(...) {
    freed->next = first_free
    first_free = freed;
}
malloc(...) {
    if (can use first free) {
        void *to_return = first_free;
        first_free = first_free->next;
        return to_return;
    }
}
vulnerable() {
    char *p = malloc(100);
    free(p);
    free(p);
    char *q = malloc(100);
    char *r = malloc(100);
    strlcpy(q, attacker_input1, 100);
    char *s = malloc(100);
    strlcpy(r, attacker_input2, 100);
    strlcpy(s, attacker_input3, 100);
}

```

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
To do memory[0x123456] $\leftarrow$ 0x789abc <br> what should input1/input2/input3 be?
:::

::::
